description:
## The change

In \`src/ir_typer.rs\`, \`process_worklist\` does:

\`\`\`rust
let caller_ft = specs.get(&spec_key).unwrap().clone();
\`\`\`

per worklist iteration, then passes \`&caller_ft\` to the walk and
to compute_return_for_spec. The clone is defensive paranoia — both
consumers take \`&specs\` (immutable) too, so the borrow checker is
happy with a plain reference. The only mutations in the iter body
are to \`effective_returns\` (a different HashMap) and to \`specs\`
ONLY at the top via \`specs.insert(spec_key.clone(), ft)\` BEFORE
the \`caller_ft\` borrow would begin.

Replace with:

\`\`\`rust
let caller_ft = specs.get(&spec_key).unwrap();
\`\`\`

If the borrow checker complains, the right fix is to restructure
(e.g., insert into \`specs\` last), not to re-add the clone.

## Why this matters

\`FnTypes\` contains \`block_envs: HashMap<BlockId, HashMap<Var, Descr>>\`
and \`vars: HashMap<Var, Descr>\` — cloned per worklist iter is
substantial waste. For a 100-pop worklist on ast_eval that's ~100
unnecessary HashMap clones per type_module invocation.

## Acceptance

The clone is gone. Tests pass. ast_eval typer perf either stays the
same (if clones weren't on the critical path) or improves (if they
were). PROV.4's counters unchanged.

## Independent of

All other PROV tickets.
title: fz-PROV.3: remove unnecessary caller_ft.clone() in process_worklist
